tam.source: Grand View Research 2025 — AI Tutors Market Report

sam.source: TAM filtered by K-12 segment (~50% of AI tutors market) and North America geographic share (~35%)

som.source: 5% of SAM ($369M) — conservative estimate for sub-$1B SAM per scoring convention

growth_source: Grand View Research — AI Tutors Market Report 2025

early_adopters.segment: Homeschool parents using Education Savings Accounts (ESAs) for edtech
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: ESA programs in 18-19 states serving 1.2M+ students, awards $4,900-$11,000/year; families actively spend on curriculum and tutoring tools

early_adopters.segment: Tech-savvy middle-income parents paying for supplemental K-12 education
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: Families spend 18% of household income on academic/extracurricular help (Care.com 2025); pay $27-68/hr for tutoring; subscribe to Khanmigo ($4/mo), Chegg ($15-20/mo)

early_adopters.segment: K-12 school districts piloting AI-powered learning tools
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: 60% of K-12 teachers using AI tools; districts like Gwinnett County (GA), Wichita (KS), Cajon Valley (CA) actively deploying AI tutoring; US Dept of Education $100M EdTech for All initiative

early_adopters.segment: After-school/supplemental learning parents seeking affordable tutoring alternatives
early_adopters.evidence_of_spending: US online private tutoring market $4.32B (2024); parents paying $35-110/hr for human tutors; Khanmigo grew from 68K to 1.4M users in one year
